title: Berkshire Hathaway Q3 2025 Earnings Release
content: OMAHA, Neb. — Berkshire Hathaway Inc. today released its third quarter 2025 earnings. Operating earnings were $14.5 billion for Q3 2025, compared with $13.7 billion in Q3 2024. Insurance-underwriting earned $1.84 billion in Q3 2025, reflecting improved underwriting discipline at GEICO and the Primary Group. GEICO's combined ratio improved to 85.3 from 96.3 a year ago, driven by rate increases and reduced frequency. Insurance-investment income was $3.66 billion, benefiting from Berkshire's large Treasury bill portfolio at elevated interest rates. BNSF Railway operating earnings were $1.48 billion (-8%), reflecting lower freight volumes and higher employee costs. Berkshire Hathaway Energy contributed $2.33 billion, though wildfire liabilities remain a watch item. Manufacturing, Service and Retailing earnings were $4.04 billion. Berkshire's equity portfolio had a fair value of approximately $272 billion at September 30, 2025. Cash, cash equivalents, and short-term Treasury bills stood at $325 billion, a record. Warren Buffett commented in the earnings note: 'We remain disciplined. At current equity valuations, we prefer to hold Treasury bills rather than overpay for partial ownership of even wonderful businesses.' Book value per Class B equivalent share was $231.40 at September 30, 2025. Berkshire has not paid a dividend since 1967. The company repurchased $0 of its own shares in Q3 2025 at current prices.
